content: I'm setting up my home office in the new house I bought in Berlin. The renovation is almost done, and I need to decide on tech setup for my journalism work.
Congratulations on the new home! What kind of work do you typically do that would influence your tech needs?

I cover technology, so I'm often testing new devices and need to run various operating systems. I also do a lot of video calls with sources. The office space has great natural light but limited wall space for monitors.
A multi-monitor setup might be challenging then. Have you considered a high-quality ultrawide monitor instead?
I have, but I'm also really into mechanical keyboards - I have three different ones I rotate through depending on my mood. I need a desk setup that can accommodate them.
That's interesting! What draws you to mechanical keyboards? And do you have specific switch preferences?

I love the tactile feedback - I have a linear red switch one for fast typing, a tactile brown for everyday use, and a clicky blue for when I'm feeling energetic. I'm also an avid birdwatcher, so I installed a window bird feeder right outside where my desk will go.

That sounds like a perfect setup - work and birdwatching combined! For your desk, you might want something with keyboard trays or adjustable height to switch between your different keyboards comfortably.
Good idea. I've been reading sci-fi novels on my Kindle during breaks - just finished The Three-Body Problem. I need good lighting for both reading physical books and working on my crossword puzzles.
A desk lamp with adjustable color temperature would be great for that. It can help reduce eye strain during long writing sessions and provide warm light for reading.
I was thinking about getting a standing desk too. I've read that alternating positions helps with focus. My budget is around €2000 for the complete setup including chair, monitors, and peripherals.
That's a reasonable budget. A quality standing desk with a good ergonomic chair would be worth the investment for your work. Have you considered any particular brands?

I've been looking at Uplift and Fully. I need something sturdy enough to hold multiple monitors and my keyboard collection. Also, I should mention I'm 56, so I need to be mindful of ergonomics for my age.
